probes.name: time_buffer_reminder_check
probes.prompt: Hey, can you check if there are any reminders or tasks that should fire in the next 2 hours? I have a dentist appointment at 15:30 and I need to leave by 14:45 to make it on time. Make sure nothing important slips through — I don't want to miss any triggered nudges just because I'll be away.
probes.criteria:
The periodic_checker agent must scan upcoming reminders/tasks for the next 2-hour window, identify any that should fire, and present them clearly. It must NOT suppress any triggered reminder even though the user mentions being away soon. It should acknowledge the dentist appointment and consider time buffers around it when advising on what needs attention now versus later.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #8 (time constraint awareness), #24 (verify tasks), and #39 (specific time framing for reminders)

probes.name: user_busy_cooldown_respect
probes.prompt: Jestem w pracy do 17:00, nie przypominaj mi o niczym mało ważnym w tym czasie. Ale jak jest coś naprawdę pilnego to mnie zawiadom. Potem po 17:00 możesz wrócić do normalnych przypomnień. Mam taski związane z master thesis i negocjacją podwyżki — te drugie są wrażliwe czasowo.
probes.criteria:
The periodic_checker agent must respect the user_busy window (until 17:00), suppress low-priority nudges during that time, but still surface any genuinely urgent items. It must distinguish between the master thesis tasks (lower urgency while busy) and the pay negotiation task (explicitly time-sensitive). After 17:00, normal reminder behavior resumes. Response should be in Polish or mixed Polish/English matching the user's language.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #8 (busy until 17:00, master thesis), #9/#19 (negotiate higher pay as high priority, time-sensitive), and the agent's core duty to respect user_busy/cooldown

probes.name: triggered_reminder_no_suppress
probes.prompt: I just realized I set a reminder to buy stocks as soon as the exchange opens and to finalize the sale before 17:00. It's now 9:05 and the exchange opened at 9:00 — did the reminder already fire? I also had a reminder for a model download at huggingface.co/edp1096/Huihui-Qwen3.5-27B-abliterated-FP8 that was supposed to go off 40 minutes ago. I want to make sure nothing was suppressed silently. Show me all reminders that should have triggered since this morning.
probes.criteria:
The periodic_checker agent must never suppress a triggered reminder. It must check for all reminders that should have fired since morning (stock purchase at exchange open ~9:00, sale finalization deadline at 17:00, and the model download ~40 min ago), surface every one of them, and explain whether each was delivered or missed. It should treat missed triggers as a problem to flag, not silently skip them.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #2 (why are you reminding me about stocks, already bought), #16 (remind in 40min for model download), and #27 (stock exchange open + finalize before 17:00)

probes.name: goal_progress_and_streak_check
probes.prompt: Hej, sprawdź moje cele i taski pogrupowane według goals. Chcę zobaczyć czy postęp się rejestruje poprawnie, bo wczoraj miałem problem z tym że goals nie rejestrowały progress. Sprawdź też czy streaki działają — dermatologa zrobiłem, 3d printed bed fixture też skończone. I czy to widać w systemie?
probes.criteria:
The periodic_checker agent must fetch goals with their associated tasks grouped, verify that progress tracking is functioning (referencing the previously reported bug with goals not registering progress), and confirm whether completed items (dermatologist appointment, 3D printed bed fixture) are reflected in the system. It should also check streak status. Response language should match the user's Polish/mixed style.

probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #12 (goals not registering progress bug), #14 (streaks, dermatologist done, 3d printed bed fixture done), #17 (check goals), and #30 (fetch tasks grouped by goals)

probes.name: event_prep_with_nudge_guidance
probes.prompt: I have a busy day tomorrow — weekend starts but I probably won't wake up until 12:00. I need to: test setting up a Flutter app for Kubiak with Claude Code, download the model from huggingface.co/edp1096/Huihui-Qwen3.5-27B-abliterated-FP8, and check the refund status for pit 28. Can you prepare a nudged schedule for tomorrow that respects my late wake-up time and spaces things out realistically? Also I should have a reminder set for the pit 28 refund — confirm that's in the system.
probes.criteria:
The periodic_checker agent must handle event prep with time buffers: account for the user's late wake-up (after 12:00), space out tasks realistically across the available afternoon, produce a single coherent nudge/guidance schedule, and confirm the pit 28 refund reminder is registered. It should not over-schedule or ignore the late start constraint.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #6 (download model task), #20 (remind in a week to check refund for pit 28), #28 (weekend starts, won't wake until 12:00, create calendar tasks), and #29 (Flutter app for Kubiak, Claude Code)
